retros.title: ECS Day 9 — Terraform Ship Retro
retros.description: We containerized the portal, wired up Postgres, and prayed to the ALB gods. How did deploy day go?

cards.content: Docker Compose came up on the first try (after the third try).
cards.author: livingdevops

cards.content: Health check endpoint actually returns healthy — rare win.
cards.author: devopscaptain

cards.content: Terraform plan output is longer than our sprint notes.
cards.author: livingdevops

cards.content: Add staging environment before prod deploy Friday.
cards.author: devopscaptain

retros.title: Kubernetes Pod Crash Bingo
retros.description: CrashLoopBackOff, OOMKilled, ImagePullBackOff — mark your cards. What blew up this sprint?

cards.content: kubectl explain saved us from YAML archaeology.
cards.author: livingdevops

cards.content: Forgot resource limits — node ran out of memory at 2am.
cards.author: devopscaptain

cards.content: ConfigMap typo took down three microservices. Classic.
cards.author: livingdevops

cards.content: Add liveness/readiness probes to every deployment.
cards.author: devopscaptain

retros.title: CI/CD Green Button Envy
retros.description: Pipelines, flaky tests, and the mythical all-green main branch. Retro the build life.

cards.content: Parallel test jobs cut pipeline time from 22 min to 8.
cards.author: livingdevops

cards.content: Integration tests fail only on Tuesdays. Still investigating.
cards.author: devopscaptain

cards.content: Cache Docker layers in CI — rebuilds are eating our souls.
cards.author: livingdevops

retros.title: Docker Image Size Intervention
retros.description: When your base image is bigger than the app. Time for multi-stage builds and alpine therapy.

cards.content: Multi-stage build dropped image from 1.2GB to 180MB.
cards.author: devopscaptain

cards.content: Someone committed node_modules to the build context. Again.
cards.author: livingdevops

cards.content: Add .dockerignore lint step to pre-commit hooks.
cards.author: devopscaptain

retros.title: Incident Post-Mortem: Who Pushed to Prod?
retros.description: Blameless retro for the deploy that made the pager go brrrr. What happened and what we learned.

cards.content: Rollback script worked — back online in 4 minutes.
cards.author: livingdevops

cards.content: No one knew who had prod deploy keys. Spreadsheet archaeology ensued.
cards.author: devopscaptain

cards.content: Require two approvals for production deploys.
cards.author: livingdevops

cards.content: Write runbook for database migration rollback.
cards.author: devopscaptain
